_purpose: Fresh, novel, ambitious prompts across all vendored libs (p5/tone/pixi/matter/d3/chart/globe/gsap/anime/three/raycast + crossovers). Distinct themes NOT in the prior 384. Run via clean_batch_driver.py.

entries.goal: Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Physarum Bloom' that simulates slime-mold growth using thousands of agents that deposit and follow a pheromone trail map, producing the eerie organic vein-networks of Physarum polycephalum. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Render 60,000+ sensor-based agents writing to an off-screen pixel buffer with a blur/decay diffusion pass each frame, so glowing dendritic filaments self-organize into branching transport networks in real time. Palette: bioluminescent acid-yellow and electric-magenta trails over a deep ink-black void, with a soft additive bloom. Interactive core: tap/click (and touch-drag on mobile) drops a food node that nearby agents swarm toward, reshaping the whole network; pinch or drag rewires the colony. On-screen controls (large touch buttons + sliders): a Sensor Angle slider (tight spirals vs wide webs), a Decay slider (ghostly vs crisp), an agent-Speed slider, a 4-way Palette cycle button (Acid / Coral Reef / Arctic Ice / Ember), and a Reset/Scatter button that re-randomizes all agents. Every control visibly transforms the living pattern instantly. Mobile-friendly: responsive full-bleed canvas, touch food-drops, controls reachable with thumbs.

Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Loom Collapse' that runs a true Wave Function Collapse tiler in real time, watching an empty grid resolve cell-by-cell into a seamless interlocking pattern of pipes, knots, and circuit-traces while you watch the entropy ripple outward. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Implement WFC over an adjacency-constrained tileset drawn procedurally on the canvas (rotatable edge-socket tiles), animating each collapse step and back-propagating constraints with a visible 'superposition shimmer' on undecided cells. Palette: a bold Bauhaus set of cobalt blue, cadmium yellow, vermilion and warm cream, switchable to a neon-on-black 'circuit' theme. Interactive core: tap any cell (touch-friendly) to force-collapse it to a chosen tile and watch the wave re-solve around your choice; drag to paint seed constraints. On-screen controls (big touch buttons): a Tileset switch (Pipes / Knots / Circuits / Truchet arcs), a Speed slider (step-by-step vs instant), a Palette toggle, a 'Collapse Now' button that finishes the whole grid in one burst, and a 'New Seed' button that restarts the weave. Every button changes the visible output. Mobile-friendly: responsive grid that fits the screen, tap-to-collapse, thumb-reachable controls.

Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Harmonograph Atelier' that draws the hypnotic looping curves of a four-pendulum harmonograph, where decaying sine oscillations on X and Y trace endlessly evolving Lissajous-rosette spirals like a Victorian drawing machine. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Continuously plot the harmonograph equation with adjustable frequencies, phases, amplitudes and damping, leaving an additive-blended glowing ink trail that thickens where lines overlap. Palette: iridescent oil-slick gradient (teal to violet to gold) stroked on warm parchment, with an alternate 'neon noir' magenta-cyan-on-black mode. Interactive core: drag anywhere (touch-drag on mobile) to nudge the pendulum frequencies live and morph the figure; double-tap to freeze the current masterpiece. On-screen controls (large sliders + buttons): two Frequency-ratio sliders, a Damping slider (tight knot vs sprawling web), a Phase slider, a Palette/background toggle, a 'Randomize' button that springs a fresh harmonic figure, and a 'Clear & Redraw' button. Every control re-shapes the curve visibly as it draws. Mobile-friendly: responsive square canvas, touch-drag retuning, thumb sliders.

Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Differential Bloom' that grows organic coral-and-brain-coral squiggles via differential growth: a closed chain of nodes that repeatedly inserts new points, repels neighbors, and seeks attraction, buckling into ever more wrinkled, lifelike convoluted curves. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Simulate a growing polyline (node insertion + repulsion + spring + alignment forces) that crinkles into intricate folded ribbons in real time, rendered with smooth curve interpolation and a subtle gradient fill that reads like cross-sections of coral or cerebral folds. Palette: living-coral pink and bone-white on deep ocean teal, with alternate 'cerebral' grey-pink and 'lichen' green-gold themes. Interactive core: tap/drag (touch) to add attraction points the growing curve creeps toward, sculpting its spread; tap to spawn a second growing colony. On-screen controls (touch buttons + sliders): a Growth-rate slider, a Repulsion-radius slider (tight wrinkles vs loose loops), a Max-nodes slider, a Palette cycle, a 'New Seed Ring' button, and a 'Freeze/Resume' toggle. Every control visibly alters how the form crinkles and spreads. Mobile-friendly: responsive canvas, touch attraction points, thumb-reachable controls.

Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Cathedral Light' that generates luminous procedural stained-glass windows from a Voronoi tessellation, with leaded black cames between jewel-toned glass panes that glow as if backlit by sunlight, complete with a slow drifting godray shimmer. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Build a Voronoi diagram from scattered seed points, render each cell as a translucent glass pane with a per-pane radial gradient and a subtle noise texture, stroke the cell borders as thick dark lead, and animate a moving light source that brightens panes it passes over. Palette: deep cathedral jewel tones - sapphire, ruby, emerald, amber and amethyst - over a warm gold backlight, switchable to a cool 'moonlit chapel' blue-silver theme. Interactive core: tap/drag (touch) to drop or move a seed point and watch the whole window re-tessellate live; drag the sun to sweep the light across the glass. On-screen controls (big touch buttons + sliders): a Pane-count slider (few bold shards vs fine mosaic), a Lead-thickness slider, a Glow-intensity slider, a Palette toggle (Cathedral / Moonlit / Art-Deco / Tropical), a 'Shatter & Regenerate' button, and a sun auto-drift toggle. Every control reshapes the glass visibly. Mobile-friendly: responsive arched canvas, touch seed editing, thumb controls.

Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Magma Lamp' that renders gooey merging-and-splitting metaball blobs like a liquid lava lamp, computing a smooth scalar field and a marching-squares contour so the blobs fuse with that signature elastic neck when they touch. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Maintain a dozen+ floating charge points whose summed inverse-distance field is threshold-contoured each frame into smooth merging isosurfaces, filled with a hot internal gradient and a glowing rim. Palette: molten lava - incandescent orange-red cores fading to deep purple, on near-black, with alternate 'toxic ooze' green, 'blue plasma', and 'candy gel' pink themes. Interactive core: drag (touch) to fling a blob around and watch others merge into it; tap to spawn a new blob, long-press to pop one. On-screen controls (large touch buttons + sliders): a Blob-count slider, a Viscosity/threshold slider (tight droplets vs sprawling goo), a Gravity/heat slider (rising vs sinking), a Palette cycle, a 'Stir' button that injects turbulence, and a 'Reset Lamp' button. Every control changes the flow visibly. Mobile-friendly: responsive tall canvas, touch flinging, thumb-reachable controls.

Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Grammar Garden' that grows lush procedural plants from L-system grammars, where typed rewrite rules unfold turtle-graphics ferns, vines, bushes and alien fronds that sway gently in a breeze and bloom flowers at the branch tips. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Expand a chosen L-system axiom+rules for N iterations and animate the turtle drawing the result branch-by-branch with tapering stroke widths, slight angle jitter per plant for natural variety, a per-frame wind sine that bends the whole plant, and procedurally placed petals/leaves. Palette: spring botanical - sap green and fresh moss with coral and saffron blossoms on a soft dawn-sky gradient, switchable to 'autumn' amber-rust, 'alien bioluminescent' cyan-violet, and 'ink sumi-e' monochrome themes. Interactive core: tap anywhere (touch) to plant a new specimen that grows where you tapped; drag to plant a whole row. On-screen controls (big touch buttons + sliders): a Grammar picker (Fern / Bush / Vine / Alien / Tree), an Iterations slider (sprout vs ancient), a Branch-angle slider, a Wind-strength slider, a Palette/season cycle, and a 'Clear Field' button. Every control changes the foliage visibly. Mobile-friendly: responsive canvas, tap-to-plant, thumb controls.

Build a p5.js generative-art mini-app titled 'Word Swarm' where a swarm of thousands of glowing particles assembles itself into any word you type, then dissolves into a turbulent flowing galaxy and re-coalesces into the next word, with particles streaking along velocity trails as they fly home to their target letterforms. Load p5.js via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Sample the pixels of rendered text to generate per-letter target positions, then steer thousands of particles toward those targets with spring + damping + curl-noise wander, leaving additive motion-blur trails; when idle they orbit in a slow nebula, and a new word re-seeds the targets so the swarm physically reforms. Palette: a glowing cyan-to-magenta plasma gradient on deep space-black with star speckles, switchable to 'gold leaf', 'emerald aurora', and 'firefly warm' themes. Interactive core: type a word in an on-screen text field (mobile keyboard friendly) and tap Morph to reshape the swarm; tap/drag on the canvas to scatter particles like swiping through smoke, then watch them regroup. On-screen controls (large touch buttons + sliders): a word input + Morph button, a Particle-count slider, a Cohesion slider (crisp text vs hazy cloud), a Turbulence slider, a Palette cycle, and a 'Scatter' button. Every control visibly affects the swarm. Mobile-friendly: responsive canvas, on-screen text entry, thumb-reachable controls.

Build a Euclidean rhythm generator called 'Orbits' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Concept: 4 concentric glowing rings on a dark midnight-blue canvas, each ring a separate Euclidean rhythm track (kick, snare, hat, clave) rendered as a circle of beat-slots; a sweeping radar hand rotates clockwise around all rings in sync with Tone.Transport and triggers any lit slot it crosses. Each track has on-screen sliders for STEPS (1-16) and PULSES (fills) and ROTATION, plus a sound picker; changing pulses instantly redistributes the lit dots evenly around the ring using the Bjorklund algorithm and you SEE them rejiggle and HEAR the polyrhythm change. Color/vibe: neon cyan, magenta, lime and amber rings against deep indigo, with a soft bloom pulse on each ring when its slot fires. Tap-to-start audio overlay. On-screen Play/Stop, global Tempo slider, and a Randomize button that picks fresh pulse counts and audibly re-grooves. Mobile-friendly: touch sliders, responsive canvas that scales rings to fit. Every control produces visible+audible change.

Build a granular-texture pad called 'Grain Cloud' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag, with Tone.GrainPlayer driving a built-in short looping tone/noise buffer (synthesized at load via Tone.Offline so no external audio file is needed). Concept: a soft luminous fog of hundreds of drifting particle-grains fills the screen; dragging your finger across a 2D XY pad scrubs the grain PLAYBACK POSITION on the X axis and grain DENSITY/SIZE on the Y axis, and the particle cloud visibly thickens, thins, stretches and shimmers to match while the texture audibly morphs. Color/vibe: dreamy aurora gradient (teal->violet->rose) with grains as glowing motes that leave fading trails. On-screen controls: DETUNE spread slider, REVERB wet slider, playback-RATE slider, and three preset buttons (Whisper / Swarm / Glacier) that smoothly animate the pad position and reshape the cloud. Tap-to-start audio overlay. Mobile-first: large touch XY pad, responsive layout, sliders work by touch. Every button audibly+visibly recalls a texture; no dead controls.

Build a chord-progression explorer called 'Chord Bloom' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.PolySynth). Concept: a 4-slot progression timeline at the bottom; tapping a slot opens a radial 'flower' wheel of diatonic chords for the chosen key (I ii iii IV V vi vii) where each petal is a chord you can audition by touch. As you build the progression, an animated garden grows above: each chord blooms a different colored flower whose petal count = number of notes and whose hue maps to the chord root, and pressing PLAY walks Tone.Transport through the four chords looping, lighting each flower as it sounds. Color/vibe: warm botanical palette on a dusk-gradient sky, gentle sway animation, glowing pollen particles on each strum. On-screen controls: KEY selector, mode toggle (major/minor), TEMPO slider, an arpeggiate-vs-block strum toggle, and a 'Surprise' button that generates a pleasing random progression and re-grows the garden audibly. Tap-to-start overlay. Mobile-friendly responsive layout, all touch. Every control changes sound and visuals.

Build an isomorphic / microtonal keyboard called 'Prism Keys' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.PolySynth with adjustable detune for microtones). Concept: a honeycomb grid of hexagonal keys in a Wicki-Hayden isomorphic layout where the same chord shape works in any position; tapping or dragging across hexes plays notes and each pressed hex erupts in a rippling prism of refracted color that radiates to its neighbors. A toggle switches between 12-TET and a true microtonal mode (19-EDO or 24-EDO) so you can hear and feel the in-between pitches, with the hex grid relabeling and recoloring to the chosen tuning. Color/vibe: iridescent stained-glass hexes on black, light refraction sparkles, neon edge glow that pulses with note velocity. On-screen controls: tuning selector (12 / 19 / 24 EDO), waveform picker, octave shift +/- buttons, a sustain/hold toggle, and a reverb-space slider. Tap-to-start audio overlay. Mobile-first: multi-touch hexes, responsive grid sizing, glide-to-play. Every control audibly+visibly changes the instrument.

Build a dub-delay jam station called 'Echo Chamber' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.FeedbackDelay + Tone.Filter + Tone.Reverb in a dub chain). Concept: a smoky basement-dub vibe where you tap big glowing pads (skank chord stab, bass note, rimshot, melodica blip) and each hit launches a comet of light that bounces and ECHOES across the screen, each repeat a dimmer ghost in sync with the tempo-synced delay so you literally watch the dub tails decay. A big central DELAY FEEDBACK dial and a FILTER SWEEP slider let you 'ride the dub' live, smearing the echoes into long psychedelic trails while a tape-wow wobble animates the visuals. Color/vibe: Rasta-warmth meets neon noir -- gold, green, red comets over deep charcoal with vignette and grain. On-screen controls: tempo-synced delay-TIME selector (1/4, 1/8 dotted, etc.), feedback dial, filter slider, reverb-wet slider, and a 'Tape Stop' button that audibly+visibly slows everything to a halt then springs back. Tap-to-start overlay. Mobile-first, big touch pads, responsive. No dead buttons.

Build an 808 bass designer called '808 Lab' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.MembraneSynth / Tone.Synth with pitch-envelope, plus Tone.Distortion and a lowpass Tone.Filter for the classic boomy/distorted 808). Concept: a sleek trap-studio workstation where a fat oscilloscope waveform of the live 808 glows and pulses in the center; a 16-step bassline sequencer runs beneath it and a mini-piano strip lets you pitch each step (with glide/slide between notes for that sliding-808 feel). Real-time sculpting knobs reshape the tone and you SEE the waveform morph -- punchier, longer, dirtier. Color/vibe: blacked-out studio with hot-magenta and electric-blue knob glow, subwoofer cone that visibly thumps on each hit, bass-driven screen shake on heavy distortion. On-screen controls: PITCH-DECAY, AMP-DECAY, DRIVE/distortion, lowpass CUTOFF, glide amount, tempo, plus per-step velocity. Buttons for Play/Stop, Clear, and 3 genre presets (Trap / Phonk / Drill) that load distinct patterns+tones audibly. Tap-to-start overlay. Mobile-first, touch sequencer + knobs, responsive. Every control changes sound and the waveform display.

Build a generative-melody-from-math toy called 'Math Melody Machine' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.Synth + Tone.Sequence). Concept: pick a mathematical sequence -- Fibonacci, primes, digits of Pi, the Collatz path of a number, or a Lorenz-attractor stream -- and the app maps the number stream onto notes of a chosen musical scale, playing it back as an evolving melody while drawing the sequence as a luminous data-curve/graph that a playhead traces in sync, so the math IS the music and the music IS the picture. Color/vibe: clean 'beautiful-data' aesthetic -- glowing line on graph paper grid, soft bloom on the active node, gradient that shifts cool->warm as the melody climbs. On-screen controls: sequence-type selector, SCALE picker (major/minor/pentatonic/dorian), root note, NOTE-RANGE/wrap slider, tempo, and a 'seed number' input (for Collatz/Lorenz) that regenerates a totally different melody+curve. Buttons: Play/Stop and Randomize-seed. Tap-to-start overlay. Mobile-first, touch controls, responsive canvas. Every control audibly+visibly transforms the melody and the curve.

Build a call-and-response beat game called 'Echo Duel' using the real Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.Players/MembraneSynth + MetalSynth for drum voices, Tone.Transport for timing). Concept: a rhythm-memory duel against a glowing AI drummer. The AI plays a short pattern across 4 big drum pads (the 'call') with each hit flashing its pad and spawning a shockwave ring; then it's YOUR turn to tap the pads back in time (the 'response'), and the game scores your timing/accuracy with floating PERFECT/GOOD/MISS judgments and a combo meter. Each round the AI's pattern grows longer and faster -- a Simon-meets-drum-circle escalation. Color/vibe: arena-stage energy, four neon drum pads (red/yellow/blue/green) on a dark stage with spotlight beams, screen pulse on the beat, particle burst and pad-glow on every accurate hit. On-screen controls: Start/New Game, difficulty selector (Chill / Groove / Shred = tempo+length), a tempo slider, and a 'Free Jam' toggle that drops scoring so you can just play the pads freely. Tap-to-start audio overlay. Mobile-first: big touch pads, responsive, on-screen everything. Every button does something visible; show score, round, and combo on screen.

Build a GPU-accelerated twin-stick arena shooter with pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag. You pilot a glowing chevron ship through a dark void lit by a pulsing neon-magenta-and-cyan grid that flexes like a trampoline under explosions. LEFT virtual stick (or WASD) moves; RIGHT virtual stick (or mouse) aims and auto-fires a stream of additive-blended plasma bolts. Waves of geometric enemies (spinning triangles, splitting squares, snaking diamonds) pour in and home toward you; killing them sprays hundreds of glowing debris sprites via a ParticleContainer so it stays 60fps even with 2000+ particles on screen. A chunky screen-shake and chromatic bloom flash on every kill, and a combo multiplier (shown top-center) climbs as you chain kills without getting hit. On-screen buttons: a big PAUSE toggle (freezes the action and dims the grid), a BOMB button that clears the screen with an expanding shockwave ring that visibly shoves every enemy and particle outward, and a RESET button that rebuilds the wave. Touch + mouse + keyboard all work; layout is responsive and the two thumbsticks anchor to the bottom corners on mobile. Score and wave counter are always visible.

Build a mesmerizing 10,000-boid starling murmuration that flocks across the whole screen using pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag, rendered through a ParticleContainer so all 10k tiny wing-sprites stay buttery 60fps. Implement real boids flocking (separation, alignment, cohesion) on a spatial grid for speed; the swarm twists, splits, and folds into shifting dusk-sky shapes against a gradient of deep indigo fading to burnt-orange horizon. The flock is colored by velocity so fast streaks glow warm amber and slow eddies cool to violet. Touch or move the mouse and a glowing predator-hawk cursor scatters the nearby boids in a realistic panic burst that ripples outward through the whole flock; tap/click drops a temporary 'roost' attractor the swarm spirals into. On-screen sliders that ACTUALLY change the live simulation: COHESION, SEPARATION, ALIGNMENT, and a SPEED slider; plus a SHAPE button that morphs the boids' target into a heart, a spiral, or a sphere, and a SCATTER button that explodes the whole flock outward then lets it re-form. FPS and live boid count shown in a corner. Fully responsive and mobile-friendly with the sliders in a tidy bottom tray.

Build an interactive FLIP/PIC particle fluid simulation in a glass tank using pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag. Tens of thousands of fluid particles obey a grid-based FLIP solver (advect on a velocity grid, transfer back to particles) so the liquid sloshes, splashes, and forms convincing waves and droplets, all drawn as additive glowing blobs through a ParticleContainer for 60fps. The fluid glows electric-teal with a luminous caustic shimmer against a near-black tank, brighter where it moves fast. Drag a finger or the mouse through the liquid to push and swirl it, leaving a turbulent wake; tilt-style GRAVITY arrows (or device tilt on mobile) pour the whole body of fluid to that side and it crashes against the wall. On-screen buttons that each do something visible: SPLASH drops a fat blob from the top that detonates a crown of droplets; SWIRL spins up a whirlpool vortex in the center; FREEZE pauses motion mid-splash so you can admire the frozen wave; DRAIN opens a hole in the floor and the fluid pours out then refills; and a COLOR button cycles teal/magma-orange/violet palettes. Particle count and FPS shown. Responsive, touch-first, controls in a bottom bar.

Build a hypnotic 'liquid portal' shader showpiece using pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag, centered on a swirling DisplacementFilter that warps a procedurally generated nebula behind a shimmering circular gateway. The portal is a ring of molten aurora-green and royal-purple energy that ripples, with a heat-haze distortion that bends starlight passing through it; the surrounding space is a slow-drifting field of glowing dust sprites in a ParticleContainer. Move the mouse or drag a finger and the displacement vortex follows, smearing and stretching the scene like looking through troubled water; tap/click anywhere to fire a ripple shockwave that visibly distorts outward across the whole canvas. On-screen controls that visibly change the live shader: an INTENSITY slider that ramps the warp from a gentle shimmer to a screen-melting maelstrom, a SPIN slider for vortex rotation speed, a SUCK button that pulls all the dust particles screaming into the portal's center then ejects them, a PALETTE button cycling aurora / inferno / deep-sea colorways, and a CALM button that smoothly settles everything back to a still glassy portal. FPS shown in a corner. Fully responsive, mobile-friendly, controls docked at the bottom.

Build an n-body 'gravity sandbox orrery' using pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag, where thousands of glowing star-dust particles fall through the gravity of the massive bodies you place, drawn via a ParticleContainer at 60fps with long additive motion trails that paint elegant looping orbit ribbons. The void is deep space-black with particles colored by speed (slow=cool blue, fast=white-hot), so stable orbits trace serene rings and slingshots streak into comet tails. Tap/click-and-drag to place a glowing massive sun and FLING it with momentum; the whole particle cloud immediately bends around it. Each placed body has a faint Roche glow and merges in a bright flash if two collide. On-screen buttons that each do something visible: SPAWN BURST injects a fresh fountain of 3000 particles, BLACK HOLE drops a dark attractor that devours nearby particles into a shrinking accretion swirl, SUPERNOVA detonates the largest body and blasts everything radially outward, TRAILS toggles the orbit-ribbon afterglow on/off, and RESET clears space back to a single calm sun with a tidy orbiting disk. Live particle count and FPS shown. Responsive and touch-friendly with controls in a bottom tray.

Build a pulse-pounding neon vector tunnel-runner using pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag: you steer a glowing wireframe glider hurtling down an endless procedurally generated hexagonal tunnel that rushes toward you with a fake-3D perspective (concentric rings scaled and z-sorted), all rendered with additive neon line-glow against pure black. The tunnel walls strobe synesthetic colors - hot pink, electric cyan, acid green - that shift as your speed climbs, and a ParticleContainer streams thousands of speed-line sparks past the camera for a hyperspace rush. Swipe left/right or use on-screen LEFT/RIGHT buttons (and arrow keys) to bank the glider around the tunnel and dodge incoming neon barrier gates and gaps; clipping a wall flashes a red shockwave and costs a life. Score climbs with distance and a BOOST button (with a cooldown meter) slams the speed up, narrows the FOV, and intensifies the color strobe for a few seconds. A PAUSE button freezes the rush and a RESTART button rebuilds the run. Speed, distance, and lives are always shown; everything is responsive, touch-first, and the controls anchor to the bottom on mobile.

Build a tactile 'magnetic iron-filings' field-line toy using pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag, where tens of thousands of tiny filing sprites in a ParticleContainer align in real time to the magnetic field of the magnets you place, tracing the classic curving field lines at a silky 60fps. The filings glow silvery-white on a slate-dark board and brighten where the field is strong; red north poles and blue south poles emit visible colored field-line halos. Drag a finger or the mouse to sweep a magnet around and watch the entire filing field snap and re-curl to follow it in a satisfying ripple; tap/click to drop a new fixed magnet. On-screen controls that visibly change things: an ADD BAR MAGNET button and an ADD POINT CHARGE button, a FLIP POLARITY button that reverses the nearest magnet (filings dramatically re-arc), a STRENGTH slider that fattens or tightens every field line live, a SHAKE button that scatters all filings then lets them re-settle into the field, and a CLEAR button that removes the magnets and lets the filings drift to rest. Live filing count and FPS shown. Fully responsive, touch-first, with the controls in a neat bottom tray.

Build a serene bioluminescent deep-sea drift scene using pixi.js v7 loaded from a normal <script src> CDN tag: a slow-pulsing colony of procedurally animated jellyfish (each a glowing bell of layered translucent arcs with rippling tentacle trails) floats upward through an inky abyss while thousands of plankton-spark particles in a ParticleContainer drift and twinkle around them at 60fps. The jellies glow cyan, magenta, and gold and emit soft additive light halos; gentle vertical currents sway the whole scene. Drag a finger or the mouse and a glowing current-wake pushes the jellies and scatters the plankton, which slowly swirl back; tap/click anywhere to send a bioluminescent ripple pulse that makes every nearby jelly flash brighter and bloom its bell in response. On-screen controls that each do something visible: a CURRENT slider that ramps the upward drift from still to a strong rising swell, a GLOW slider that dials the bloom from dim to dazzling, a SPAWN JELLY button that births a new color-randomized jelly with a little birth-flash, a STARTLE button that makes the whole colony pulse-flash and dart in unison, and a CALM button that settles everything to a slow hypnotic float. FPS shown in a corner. Fully responsive, touch-first, controls docked at the bottom.

Build 'Chain Reaction Factory', a Rube-Goldberg contraption builder using the REAL Matter.js physics engine loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag. Warm brass-and-copper steampunk vibe on a deep navy workshop grid with glowing amber gears. The player drags pre-made gizmo parts from a left-side palette onto the canvas -- a ramp, a domino row, a see-saw lever, a spinning paddle wheel, a bucket-on-a-pulley, a cannon, and a bell at the goal -- then hits a big PLAY button to drop the starter marble and watch the whole machine fire in a satisfying cascade until it rings the bell. Every palette button spawns its gizmo; a RESET button clears the canvas; a SAVE/LOAD slot pair stores the contraption in localStorage; a STEP toggle slows physics to half-speed so you can debug. Show a live part counter and a 'goal reached' celebration with sparks when the bell rings. Mobile-friendly: touch-drag to place parts, large on-screen buttons, responsive canvas that fills the viewport with walls that reposition on resize, and a body cap (~120 parts) so it never grinds. A normal Matter.js <script src> CDN tag must be used (do not hand-roll physics).

Build 'Jelly Lab', a squishy soft-body playground using the REAL Matter.js physics engine loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag, building deformable blobs from grids of small circles wired together with stiff-but-springy constraints (Matter.Composites.softBody). Candy-bright neon vibe: translucent lime, magenta, and cyan jelly blobs that wobble and jiggle on a dark glossy floor with a subtle reflection. Tap or click anywhere to drop a new jelly blob; drag a blob with the mouse/finger and it stretches and snaps back; an on-screen WOBBLINESS slider live-changes constraint stiffness so jellies go from firm gummy to floppy slime; a SIZE slider sets blob resolution; a GRAVITY FLIP button sends them bouncing to the ceiling; a SHAKE button jolts the whole world; a CLEAR button removes all blobs. Render the blobs as smooth filled gradient shapes (not just dots) so they look gooey, with a soft glow. Mobile-friendly: multi-touch drag, big slider controls, responsive canvas with walls that reposition on resize, and a body cap so the screen never overloads. A normal Matter.js <script src> CDN tag must be used (do not hand-roll physics).
